title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Relational Lens: Materialized Results
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql materialized results increment in a versioned read model and audited transition design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 103. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects. A defensible solution applies Materialized Results, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a versioned read model and audited transition design.

content.code:
CREATE TEMP VIEW open_ticket_queue AS
SELECT ticket_id, team_id, owner_id, severity, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved';
SELECT ticket_id, severity
FROM open_ticket_queue
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Materialized Results evidence · Theory Materialized Results
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-materialized-results-14yebv" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; view, trigger, reusable-logic, or side-effect evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Materialized Results evidence · Theory Materialized Results.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Materialized Results into observable performance evidence. For Build Materialized Results evidence · Theory Materialized Results, keep Materialized Results connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-materialized-results-14yebv
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id,
       CASE WHEN status = 'resolved' THEN resolved_at ELSE opened_at END AS relevant_time
FROM tickets ORDER BY relevant_time, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
